questions.prompt: You have 90 minutes and have spent 45 of them deeply investigating one buggy checkout calculation, finding 2 bugs. The signup, login, and cart flows are untouched. What is the best move?
options: Keep going on checkout since you are finding bugs there
Move on to the untouched flows to maximise coverage before time runs out
Stop testing and start polishing the wording of your two reports
Re-test the checkout calculation a third time to be certain
questions.explanation: A time-boxed hunt rewards coverage. Whole untested flows likely hold easy bugs; sinking more time into one screen risks missing the pass bar.

questions.prompt: The manifest rates a bug as 'Critical'. To have your report count, your severity must be within one level. Which rating would FAIL the tolerance check?
options: Critical
Major
Minor
Blocker (treated as adjacent to Critical)
questions.explanation: Minor is more than one level away from Critical, so it falls outside the tolerance. Critical, an adjacent Major, or an adjacent Blocker are acceptable.

questions.prompt: Which report is most likely to be marked as a non-match against the manifest?
options: Quantity field accepts -1 and the cart shows a negative total
The 'Remember me' checkbox is unchecked by default, which you find inconvenient
Checkout succeeds with an empty cart and a $0 order is created
An invalid email like 'a@@b' is accepted at signup
questions.explanation: A default checkbox state you simply dislike is intended behaviour, not a seeded defect. The other three are real functional faults a manifest would list.

questions.prompt: Halfway through the hunt you find a layout glitch you are sure you already reported under a different title. What should you do?
options: File it again with the clearer title to be safe
Skip it; one defect should have exactly one report
File it and note in the description that it may be a duplicate
Delete your earlier report and file this new one
questions.explanation: Duplicates do not count and waste your limited time. One defect, one report; trust your running bug list rather than refiling.

questions.prompt: Which preparation step best protects your score during the timed hunt?
options: Memorising the full list of 18 bug titles beforehand
Planning a route across every flow plus a fixed reporting checklist applied to each bug
Writing all reports from memory in the last 10 minutes
Testing only the screens where you expect the hardest bugs
questions.explanation: A route guarantees coverage and a checklist keeps every report repro-complete with sane severity. Writing from memory at the end loses detail, and you cannot know the bug titles in advance.
